notes: POST-H-002 closes the local maturity dashboard based on POST-H-EVAL-001 evidence and exposes a maturity-dashboard quality gate.
POST-H-003 closes Test Contract Registry 2.0 as implemented-initial: schema v2, migration, validator, profiles, impact analyzer v2 and quality-gate signal are available locally.

POST-H-004 closes the Policy/MIASI semantic validator ampliado as implemented-initial: semantic validator, report schema, approval/RBAC/security guards, observability/evals/test-contract checks and quality-gate subgate are available locally.
POST-H-005 is the next prioritized P0 hito: Architecture map executable / dependency ownership.
This file is source-controlled project metadata, not runtime state; it must not contain secrets.

POST-H-005 closes Architecture map executable / dependency ownership as implemented-initial: schema, AST inventory, dependency graph, hotspot analyzer, ownership validation, final ArchitectureMap report and quality-gate subgate are available locally.
POST-H-006 is the next prioritized hito: CLI command registry y desacoplamiento de handlers.
POST-H-006-A starts CLI command registry as implemented-initial: static AST inventory, schema-backed registry report and read-only CLI evidence are available locally without migrating handlers.

POST-H-006-B adds the initial declarative CLI registry overlay: governed groups, descriptor coverage metrics and legacy-unregistered command visibility are available locally without handler migration.

POST-H-006-C adds incremental static handler migration for workspace.init, workspace.status and validate docs/contracts/all through cli_commands modules while preserving cli.py public wrappers and disabling runtime registry routing.

POST-H-006-D adds a read-only CLI hotspot and command ownership report: migrated/registered_only/legacy counts, side-effect/risk/domain ownership metrics and TCR association gaps are available locally for POST-H-006-E and POST-H-007 planning.

POST-H-006-E adds a local no-growth CLI gate: commands that appear as legacy-unregistered must be covered by a temporary source-controlled allowlist, while new public commands require declarative descriptors or migrated handlers.

POST-H-006 closes CLI command registry y desacoplamiento de handlers as implemented-initial: static inventory, declarative registry, migrated handlers, hotspot ownership and no-growth gate are available locally.

POST-H-007-A starts ApplicationService boundary hardening as implemented-initial: a read-only boundary/bypass inventory report identifies ApplicationService operations, API/UI mappings and CLI direct-core bypasses without runtime behavior changes.

POST-H-007-B adds a schema-valid ApplicationOperationCatalog: operations declare risk, writes, policy_required, dry_run_default, test coverage and explicit CLI/API/UI mappings without runtime behavior changes.

POST-H-007-C adds priority DTO normalization: 11 operations execute through ApplicationRequest/ApplicationResponse while preserving CommandResult exit_code, findings, data/report_paths and metadata without adding public CLI commands or HTTP routes.

POST-H-007-D adds initial ApplicationService boundary policy: InterfaceClient allowlists, API/UI strict exposure checks, sensitive-operation PolicyEngine evaluation and dry-run guardrails without adding public CLI commands or HTTP routes.

POST-H-007-E adds initial CLI registry/ApplicationService integration: selected CommandDescriptor metadata can point to operation_id, API/UI operation contracts are checked, and quality-gate hardening includes an application-cli-boundary-integration subgate without enabling runtime registry routing.

POST-H-007 closes ApplicationService boundary hardening as implemented-initial: boundary inventory, operation catalog, DTO normalization, interface policy and CLI registry/ApplicationService quality-gate integration are available locally.

POST-H-008-A starts Runtime state lifecycle policy as implemented-initial: runtime artifact taxonomy, RuntimeStatePolicy/RuntimeStateInventory schemas and .devpilot/runtime_state_policy.json are available locally without cleanup execution.

POST-H-008-B adds read-only runtime state inventory: policy-driven scanner, class summaries, versioned runtime artifact detection and JSON/Markdown reports are available locally without cleanup/export execution.

POST-H-008-C adds dry-run-first runtime cleanup planning: artifacts are grouped as safe-cleanup/requires-approval/never-delete/retained, source-of-truth paths are protected, and cleanup --execute requires explicit confirmation.

POST-H-008-D adds local redacted runtime evidence export: runtime-state export dry-run/execute, RuntimeStateExportManifest, checksums, SecretGuard redaction and metadata-only binary/local DB handling are available without network or external APIs.

POST-H-008-E closes Runtime state lifecycle policy as implemented-initial: runtime-state-hygiene blocks dirty runtime artifacts in source/release archives, checks git archive HEAD when available, falls back to a deterministic source archive plan for clean ZIPs, and integrates with quality-gate hardening/industrial.

POST-H-009-A starts Documentation governance y canonical sources as implemented-initial: DocumentationSourceRegistry, DocumentationGovernanceReport schemas, canonical source registry and documentation governance baseline are available locally without validator CLI or quality-gate integration yet.

POST-H-009-B adds Documentation governance validator as implemented-initial: docs-governance validate checks canonical registry paths, owner/status_required, approved frontmatter, doc_id/status consistency and required_tests without drift checks, network, external APIs or LLM judge.

POST-H-009-C adds Documentation governance sync validation as implemented-initial: roadmap Markdown/JSON version, milestones, decisions, closure status and next hito checks are available locally through docs-governance validate/report.

POST-H-009-D adds Documentation governance backlog governance as implemented-initial: roadmap-derived executable backlogs POST-H-002..POST-H-025 are registered/governed, backlog naming/frontmatter/milestone/priority checks are available through docs-governance validate/report, with missing future backlog files treated as planned info rather than blocking drift.

POST-H-009-E closes Documentation governance y canonical sources as implemented-initial: docs-governance is integrated into quality-gate hardening/industrial as a read-only deterministic subgate, TCR v1/v2 is registered, and the canonical docs update/runbook procedure is documented without enabling network, external APIs, LLM judge or source mutations.

POST-H-010-A starts Observability retention local as implemented-initial: ObservabilityRetentionPolicy schema, local retention_policy.json defaults, semantic loader/validator and tests are available without inventory, cleanup, export or quality-gate integration yet.

POST-H-010-B adds Observability inventory read-only as implemented-initial: ObservabilityInventory schema, metadata-only inventory builder, CLI observability inventory, optional reports and tests are available without cleanup, export, redaction execution or quality-gate integration yet.

POST-H-010-C adds Observability cleanup plan dry-run as implemented-initial: ObservabilityCleanupPlan schema, planner, CLI observability cleanup-plan, PolicyEngine simulations and optional reports are available without cleanup/export/redaction execution or source mutations.

POST-H-010-D adds local redacted observability export as implemented-initial: ObservabilityRedactedExport schema, exporter, CLI observability export, SecretGuard-backed redaction, checksums and optional reports/audit_exports are available without raw prompts, raw outputs, secrets, SQLite raw export, network or remote export.

POST-H-010-E closes Observability retention local as implemented-initial: ObservabilityRetentionHygiene schema, read-only hygiene gate, observability-retention quality-gate subgate and dedicated runbook are available without runtime-output dependency, network, external APIs, cleanup execution or source mutations.
POST-H-011 is the next prioritized hito: RAG groundedness evals.

POST-H-011-A starts RAG groundedness evals as implemented-initial: RagGroundednessEval/RagGroundednessReport schemas and the rag_groundedness_post_h_cases fixture define local expected sources, required claims and forbidden claims without web search, external APIs, LLM judge, remote execution, connector write or plugin execution.

POST-H-011-B adds local citation extraction and source coverage for RAG groundedness fixtures as implemented-initial: paths/doc_id/headings/snippets are normalized locally; source_coverage detects missing, runtime, remote and stale sources without web search, external APIs, LLM judge or mutations.

POST-H-011-C adds deterministic RAG claim groundedness evaluation: required_claims, forbidden_claims, claim_support and unsupported_claims are evaluated locally without LLM judge, web search or external APIs.

POST-H-011-D adds RAG groundedness eval runner integration as implemented-initial: rag groundedness-eval CLI, eval run --suite rag-groundedness bridge, local RAG query integration and outputs/evals report writing are available without network, external APIs, LLM judge, remote execution, connector write or plugin execution.

POST-H-011-E closes RAG groundedness evals as implemented-initial: rag-groundedness-ready is bound to hardening/industrial quality-gate profiles, verifies local suite/source/claim/query readiness and negative forbidden-claim blocking without network, external APIs, web search, LLM judge, remote execution, connector write, plugin execution or versioned outputs/evals.
POST-H-012 is the next prioritized hito: Approval/RBAC hardening.

POST-H-012-A approves the Approval/RBAC hardening backlog and adds a declarative SensitiveActionCatalog schema/catalog/validator as implemented-initial; later micro-sprints cover binding, exposure, enforcement and quality gate.
POST-H-012-A keeps remote execution, connector write and plugin execution disabled and non-executable.

POST-H-012-B adds strong approval binding as implemented-initial: approval_id can be checked against exact actor_id, role_at_decision, tool_id, action, subject, subject_hash, command_id and tool_call_id without enabling sensitive execution.
POST-H-012-B keeps RBAC exposure, homogeneous PolicyEngine enforcement and approval-rbac quality gate planned for POST-H-012-C/D/E; C and D are now covered, E remains pending.

POST-H-012-C adds RBAC exposure reporting as implemented-initial: identity exposure builds a read-only actor/role/action/interface/effect matrix from Identity Registry, SensitiveActionCatalog and MIASI policy matrix, with optional outputs/reports evidence and without granting permissions, network, external APIs, remote execution, connector write, plugin execution or source mutations.

POST-H-012-D adds homogeneous PolicyEngine enforcement as implemented-initial: PolicyEngine now evaluates SensitiveActionCatalog, strong approval binding, exact RBAC required-role checks and interface blocking with normalized APPROVAL_REQUIRED, RBAC_DENIED and APPROVAL_SCOPE_MISMATCH findings, without enabling remote execution, connector write, plugin execution, external APIs or destructive mutations.

POST-H-012-E closes Approval/RBAC hardening as implemented-initial: approval-rbac-hardening is bound to hardening/industrial quality-gate profiles, validates SensitiveActionCatalog, RBAC exposure, PolicyEngine scenarios, strong approval binding and approval lifecycle docs without enabling remote execution, connector write, plugin execution, external APIs or destructive mutations.
POST-H-013 is the next prioritized hito: Audit pack integrity.
POST-H-013-A starts Audit pack integrity with manifest v2 schemas and local policy; builder/verifier/crypto remain future micro-sprints.
POST-H-013-A keeps last_completed_sprint=POST-H-012 because the POST-H-013 backlog is active but not closed.
POST-H-013-A starts Audit pack integrity as implemented-initial: AuditPackManifestV2, AuditPackIntegrityReport and local audit pack policy are available.

POST-H-013-B adds AuditPackV2Builder with dry-run default, execute mode under outputs/auditpacks, SHA-256 file checksums and mandatory redaction report; signing, encryption and verify-v2 remain pending.

POST-H-013-C adds AuditPackV2Verifier with local manifest schema validation, manifest self-hash verification, SHA-256 file verification, missing-file detection, extra-member detection and integrity report generation under outputs/auditpacks; optional signing and encryption are implemented in POST-H-013-D.

POST-H-013-D adds optional local crypto for audit packs: HMAC-SHA256 sidecar signing, optional Fernet encryption when available, verify-v2 crypto status reporting, no remote KMS and no repo-stored keys.

POST-H-013-E closes Audit pack integrity as implemented-initial: audit-pack-integrity is bound to hardening/industrial quality-gate profiles and validates policy, no-go gates, redaction report, build-v2 dry-run, TCR registration and no-certification docs without writing packs or enabling network, external APIs, KMS, remote execution, connector write or plugin execution.
POST-H-014 is the next prioritized hito: UI/API industrial shell.
POST-H-014-A approves UI/API industrial shell and adds ApiRouteContractRegistry for local FastAPI route inventory.
POST-H-014-B is the next micro-sprint: Response mapping y errores homogéneos.
POST-H-014-B adds homogeneous response mapping for the local UI/API shell: CommandResult/ApplicationResponse HTTP mapping, 422 validation errors and redacted technical exception payloads.
POST-H-014-C is the next micro-sprint: UI Route Contract y shell de producto.
POST-H-014-C adds UI Route Contract Registry and local product shell badges/states for Dashboard, Reports, Traces, Approvals and Settings.
POST-H-014-D is the next micro-sprint: Security hardening local de API/UI.
POST-H-014-D adds local API/UI security hardening: protected security posture endpoint, CORS sanitization, non-local bind guard and UI secret redaction checks.

POST-H-014-E closes UI/API industrial shell as implemented-initial: ui-api-industrial-shell quality subgate, schema-backed report, API/UI registry validation, Web UI smoke and operational docs are integrated into hardening/industrial quality gates.
POST-H-015 is the next prioritized hito: Local operator dashboard.

POST-H-015-A approves Local operator dashboard and adds OperatorDashboardSnapshot schema, local dashboard_config, source_refs requirements and CLI-valid fixture without implementing aggregator/API/UI yet.

POST-H-015-B adds OperatorDashboardAggregator read-only local signal aggregation, optional outputs/reports/operator_dashboard_snapshot JSON/Markdown writing and required-source blocking without ApplicationService/API/UI exposure yet.
POST-H-015-B closes after correcting TestContractRegistryV2 domain drift: operator.dashboard was not a valid domain enum and is mapped to product.ui.

POST-H-015-C adds OperatorDashboardApplicationService and protected GET /api/v1/operator/dashboard through ApplicationService/ApplicationResponse without enabling UI, remote execution, connector write, plugin execution or external APIs.

POST-H-015-D adds the Web UI Operator Dashboard inside ui.dashboard, consuming api.operator.dashboard through DevPilotApiClient and showing source_refs, no-go gates and local/dry-run next actions without frontend filesystem reads.
POST-H-015-E closes Local operator dashboard with operator-dashboard-ready quality gate and operational runbook.
POST-H-015-D closure required correcting PostHManifest field drift and TestContractRegistryV2 enum/null drift before advancing.
POST-H-016 is the next prioritized hito: Workspace portfolio hardening.
POST-H-016-A approves Workspace portfolio hardening and adds MultiworkspaceRegistryV2 schema, read-only v1 to v2 migration and registry-validate v2 CLI without mutating the v1 registry.
POST-H-016-A closure corrected PostHManifest next_sprint and TCR v1 scope drift before advancing.
POST-H-016-B adds WorkspaceIsolationValidator and workspace isolation-check CLI as read-only metadata validation for root/state/outputs/traces boundaries without reading secrets or state DBs.

POST-H-016-C hardens portfolio status over Workspace Registry v2 plus WorkspaceIsolationValidator, reporting only registered workspaces, unknown operational sources and no-go metadata without reading secrets or state DBs.
POST-H-016-D adds secure CLI/API integration for portfolio status through ApplicationService and protected GET /api/v1/portfolio/status without active workspace mutation.

POST-H-016-E closes Workspace portfolio hardening as implemented-initial: workspace-portfolio-hardening is bound to hardening/industrial quality gates, validates registry v2, isolation, portfolio status, operation catalog, api.portfolio.status and onboarding docs without enabling remote execution, connector write, plugin execution, network or external APIs.
POST-H-017 is the next prioritized hito: Release reproducibility pack.
POST-H-017-A approves Release reproducibility pack and adds schema-backed ReleaseReproducibilityPack plus ReleaseEnvironmentSnapshot contracts.
POST-H-017-A adds local release reproducibility policy with runtime exclusions, dirty-repo block semantics, secret-free evidence requirements and dry-run-only safety flags.
POST-H-017-B is next: redacted environment snapshot generation without reading .env or secret values.
POST-H-017-B adds redacted ReleaseEnvironmentSnapshotBuilder and release environment-snapshot CLI without reading .env files, secret values, network resources or external APIs.
POST-H-017-B was verified as closable based on focused tests, generated redacted environment snapshot, schema validation, docs governance, TCR v1/v2 and hardening quality gate evidence.

POST-H-017-C adds SourceArchiveManifestBuilder, ReleaseSourceArchiveManifest schema and release source-archive-manifest CLI with critical artifact SHA-256 checksums and forbidden archive entry detection without network, external APIs, remote execution, connector write, plugin execution or secret reads.

POST-H-017-D adds ReleaseReproducibilityVerifier, ReleaseReproducibilityVerification schema and release reproducibility-verify CLI to validate local packs, dirty=false, secret-free safety flags, generated environment/source archive evidence and critical checksums without publishing, deploying, network, external APIs, remote execution, connector write or plugin execution.

POST-H-017-E adds ReleaseReproducibilityPackBuilder, release reproducibility-pack CLI and release-reproducibility quality subgate to generate and verify local dry-run release reproducibility evidence without publishing, deploying, network, external APIs, remote execution, connector write, plugin execution or source mutations.
POST-H-017-E closes Release reproducibility pack as implemented-initial; future evolution may add formal local signing, attestation and release promotion workflow.
POST-H-018 is the next prioritized hito: Connector sandbox avanzado.

POST-H-018-A approves Connector sandbox avanzado and adds ConnectorSandboxPolicy, ConnectorReplayFixture and ConnectorSandboxReport schemas plus deny-write connector_sandbox_policy.json without enabling connector write, network, external APIs, remote execution or plugin execution.

POST-H-018-B adds ConnectorSandboxRunner and connector sandbox run CLI for local validate/dry-run/replay simulation with PolicyEngine invocation and ConnectorSandboxReport evidence, without connector write, network, external APIs, remote execution or plugin execution.

POST-H-018-C adds ConnectorReplayRunner, deterministic local connector_replay_cases fixtures, redaction checks and fixture-backed replay summaries in connector sandbox reports, without connector write, network, external APIs, remote execution, plugin execution or real connector execution.

POST-H-018-D adds ConnectorPolicyBindingValidator and connector sandbox exposure reporting: connector.validate/replay/write_future policy rules, ApprovalPolicyChecker checks for side-effecting connector classifications, RBAC evaluation for high/critical connectors and deny/block evidence for connector.write_future remain local-first and implemented-initial.

POST-H-018-E adds ConnectorSandboxQualityGate and integrates connector-sandbox into quality-gate hardening/industrial profiles without enabling connector write, network, external APIs, remote execution or plugin execution.
POST-H-018-E adds the approved connector sandbox runbook and threat model with no-go gates for read-only/replay/dry-run operation.

POST-H-018 closes Connector sandbox avanzado as implemented-initial; future connector write or external integration requires ADR, threat model, approvals, RBAC, observability and additional quality gates.
POST-H-019 is the next prioritized hito: Plugin sandbox design sin ejecución arbitraria.

POST-H-019-A approves Plugin sandbox design sin ejecución arbitraria and adds plugin_sandbox_threat_model plus plugin_sandbox_design as metadata-only/validator-only architecture without enabling plugin execution, importlib, subprocess, network, external APIs, pip install, marketplace or remote execution.
POST-H-019-B is the next micro-sprint: Permission model y manifest hardening.
POST-H-019-B adds PluginPermissionModel schema and .devpilot/plugins/plugin_permission_model.json with deny-by-default allowlist/denylist semantics.
POST-H-019-B hardens Plugin Registry manifest validation so unknown permissions and denied execution permissions such as plugin.code.execute are blocked.
POST-H-019-C adds PluginStaticValidator for metadata-only install dry-run over registered plugin manifests without loading code or reading arbitrary entrypoints.
POST-H-019-C adds PluginExposureReporter, PluginSandboxDesignReport schema and plugin dry-run --all --dry-run --write-report to produce exposure evidence with execution_allowed_total=0.

POST-H-019-D adds plugin-sandbox-design quality subgate to hardening/industrial profiles without enabling plugin execution, importlib, subprocess, network, external APIs, pip install, marketplace or filesystem write.
POST-H-019-D validates PluginRegistry, PluginPermissionModel, PluginExposureReporter and plugin-ecosystem fixture signal as implemented-initial quality-gate evidence.

POST-H-019-E closes Plugin sandbox design sin ejecución arbitraria as implemented-initial: metadata-only registry, permission model, static validator, dry-run exposure report, quality gate, runbook and ADR trigger are synchronized without enabling plugin execution.
POST-H-019 remains metadata-only; future plugin execution requires ADR, sandbox, RBAC, approvals, tests, observability and rollback.
POST-H-019-E fixes inherited TCR v2 enum drift: post-h-019-plugin-sandbox-design uses execution_profile=release instead of invalid hardening.
POST-H-020 is the next prioritized hito: Compliance mapping packs ampliados.
POST-H-020-A approves Compliance mapping packs ampliados and starts local non-certifying control/evidence mapping as implemented-initial.
POST-H-020-A adds ComplianceControlMapping, ComplianceEvidenceMapping and ComplianceMappingReport schemas with certification_claimed=false and legal_advice_claimed=false.

POST-H-020-A adds .devpilot/compliance/control_mappings.json and evidence_mappings.json for local engineering evidence without external audit, legal advice, network, APIs, remote execution or plugin execution.
POST-H-020-B is the next micro-sprint: Compliance mapping validator.

POST-H-020-B adds ComplianceMappingValidator for semantic validation of local compliance mappings without collecting evidence, generating reports, network, external APIs, certification or legal advice.
POST-H-020-B extends compliance mapping baseline with agentic domain coverage for RAG/evals safety evidence.
POST-H-020-C is the next micro-sprint: Evidence collector y report generator local.
POST-H-020-C adds ComplianceEvidenceCollector for local metadata-only inspection of declared evidence source_paths without executing source_command values.
POST-H-020-C adds ComplianceMappingReporter and compliance mapping report CLI to generate non-certifying local reports under outputs/reports by default, with explicit output paths supported.
POST-H-020-C keeps certification_claimed=false, legal_advice_claimed=false, commands_executed=false, network_used=false and external_api_used=false.
POST-H-020-D is the next micro-sprint: Integración con audit packs y quality gate.
POST-H-020-D adds ComplianceMappingQualityGate and quality-gate compliance-mapping-pack subgate for hardening/industrial profiles.
POST-H-020-D adds non-certifying compliance_mapping summary metadata to AuditPackV2 manifests without exporting evidence or claiming certification.
POST-H-020-D integrates compliance-pack-integrity eval fixture signal into the compliance mapping quality gate.
POST-H-020-E adds compliance mapping runbook and mandatory no-certification/no-legal-advice disclaimers.
POST-H-020-E documents mapped/partial/gap/not-applicable interpretation and safe gap handling.
POST-H-020-E fixes Test Contract Registry v2 classification_status values for POST-H-020-C/D contracts to explicit.

POST-H-020 closes Compliance mapping packs ampliados as implemented-initial: schemas, registries, validator, collector, reporter, CLI, quality gate, AuditPackV2 summary, runbook and disclaimers are available locally without certification claims.
POST-H-021 is the next prioritized hito after compliance mapping closure.
POST-H-021 is the next prioritized hito: Remote Runner ADR-2.
POST-H-021-A approves Remote Runner ADR-2 as design-only and adds RemoteReadinessCriteria.
POST-H-021-A inventories remote runner baseline and keeps remote execution disabled.
POST-H-021-B adds ADR-POSTH-004 Remote Runner ADR-2 as approved design-only decision.
POST-H-021-B keeps remote_execution_allowed=false and remote_runner_enabled=false.
POST-H-021-B rejects enable-now, SSH ad hoc, connector-as-runner and plugin-as-runner.
POST-H-021-C adds RemoteReadinessChecker and RemoteReadinessReporter for local read-only readiness evidence.
POST-H-021-C registers RemoteReadinessReport and remote runner readiness CLI without enabling remote execution.
POST-H-021-D is the next micro-sprint: Quality gate remote disabled.
